title: Deep audit: 3 math/logic bugs fixed in retirement_mc, sector_rotation, technical_analysis

bugs_found: retirement_mc.py: Student-t(df=8) has variance 8/6 not 1.0 — raw standard_t inflates simulation volatility by 15.5%, significantly depressing median outcomes over 30-year horizons (up to 14% less wealth for aggressive allocations)

sector_rotation.py: Momentum direction ratio r1m/(r3m/3) produces inverted labels when both returns are negative — moderating declines labeled as 'decelerating' and deepening declines labeled as 'accelerating'
technical_analysis.py: SMA 200 computed with min(200, len-1) window — when <200 days of data, a shorter-period SMA masquerades as SMA 200, producing misleading trend direction signals
observation.gap_identified: Math correctness in simulation and signal engines — subtle distribution/sign bugs that produce plausible-looking but incorrect outputs

bugs_fixed: retirement_mc.py: Normalize t-distribution by sqrt((df-2)/df) so sigma*t(df) has variance sigma^2 (applied in both simulate_retirement and compute_safe_withdrawal_rate)
sector_rotation.py: Extract _classify_momentum_direction() function; flip accelerating/decelerating interpretation when trend is negative (same magnitude comparison, correct sign handling)
technical_analysis.py: Only compute SMA 200 when len(close) >= 200; return empty Series otherwise so _safe_last returns None, preventing fake SMA 200 from polluting trend signals
implementation.feature_built: Extracted _classify_momentum_direction as testable unit function in sector_rotation.py

limitations: Retirement MC fix changes simulation outputs — safe withdrawal rate numbers will shift (correct direction: less pessimistic)
Sector rotation fix only tested via unit function, not end-to-end with live data
Did not audit all 55 services — focused on 8 most complex ones
assessment.self_critique: The retirement_mc t-distribution bug has been present since the service was created — should have been caught by calibration tests comparing simulated vs theoretical volatility. Would add such a test for all MC-based services.

next_steps: Add MC volatility calibration tests for monte_carlo.py and crash_timeline.py (same t-distribution pattern may exist)
Audit remaining un-audited services: bubble_detector, systemic_risk, insider_trading
Add property-based tests for sign-sensitive calculations across all signal components
